description: Granular skill taxonomy for IELTS Listening, derived from official IELTS Listening Band Descriptors and question-type performance criteria across all four parts.

categories.description: Accurately capturing specific facts, numbers, names and details from audio

skills.description: Accurately recording specific facts, numbers, dates and names heard in the audio

ranks.1: Frequently mishears or misses specific details; poor accuracy on numbers and names.
ranks.2: Captures obvious details in slow, clear speech but misses details in natural pace audio.
ranks.3: Reliably captures most specific details; occasional errors with fast speech or accents.
ranks.4: Consistently accurate on specific details across all four parts and accent types.
ranks.5: Perfect detail accuracy — captures all specific information including in fast, accented speech.

skills.description: Accurately completing forms, notes and tables with information from the audio

ranks.1: Cannot match audio information to form fields; fills in wrong sections or leaves blanks.
ranks.2: Completes obvious form fields but misses information requiring prediction or inference.
ranks.3: Completes most form fields accurately; occasional spelling errors or missed items.
ranks.4: Consistently accurate form completion with correct spelling and appropriate format.
ranks.5: Perfect form completion — accurately captures and formats all required information.

skills.description: Accurately capturing numbers, prices, dates, times and spelled-out words

ranks.1: Frequently mishears numbers and cannot follow spelled words.
ranks.2: Captures simple numbers but struggles with large figures, decimals or spelled sequences.
ranks.3: Reliably captures most numbers and spellings at normal pace.
ranks.4: Consistently accurate with all number formats and letter-by-letter spelling.
ranks.5: Perfect accuracy on all numerical and spelling information at any speed.

categories.description: Understanding the overall purpose, topic and main points of audio passages

skills.description: Identifying the main topic, purpose or argument of a spoken passage

ranks.1: Cannot identify the main idea; focuses on individual words rather than overall meaning.
ranks.2: Identifies the general topic but cannot state the main argument or purpose.
ranks.3: Correctly identifies the main idea in most passages; occasional errors in Part 4.
ranks.4: Consistently identifies the main idea and purpose across all four parts.
ranks.5: Expert main idea identification — instantly grasps purpose and argument in all audio types.

skills.description: Identifying why a speaker says something and their attitude or opinion

ranks.1: Cannot identify speaker purpose or attitude; misses all opinion and attitude markers.
ranks.2: Identifies obvious opinions but misses hedging, irony or implicit attitude.
ranks.3: Identifies speaker purpose and main attitude in most contexts.
ranks.4: Consistently identifies purpose, attitude and degree of certainty across all parts.
ranks.5: Expert identification of all speaker stances including subtle attitude and implicit purpose.

categories.description: Drawing conclusions from implied meaning in spoken English

skills.description: Drawing logical conclusions from what is implied but not explicitly stated in the audio

ranks.1: Cannot make inferences from audio; only answers based on explicitly stated information.
ranks.2: Makes simple inferences from clear context but fails with subtle implied meaning.
ranks.3: Draws reasonable inferences in most contexts; struggles with Part 3 abstract discussion.
ranks.4: Consistently draws accurate inferences including from complex academic discussions.
ranks.5: Expert audio inference — correctly interprets all implied meaning across all four parts.

skills.description: Avoiding incorrect answers that are mentioned in the audio but are not the correct response

ranks.1: Frequently selects distractor answers; cannot distinguish correct answers from distractors.
ranks.2: Avoids obvious distractors but is misled by information that is later corrected.
ranks.3: Resists most distractors; occasionally misled by information that contradicts the answer.
ranks.4: Consistently identifies and ignores distractors including subtle corrections and changes.
ranks.5: Expert distractor resistance — never misled by incorrect options or false information.

categories.description: Effective use of prediction, keyword tracking and note-taking while listening

skills.description: Using questions and context to predict the type of answer before hearing it

ranks.1: Does not predict answer type; surprised by question formats and misses answers.
ranks.2: Predicts answer type for simple questions but not for complex multi-part items.
ranks.3: Effectively predicts answer type for most question formats.
ranks.4: Consistently predicts both answer type and probable content before listening.
ranks.5: Expert prediction — accurately anticipates answer type and content for all question formats.
